analysis_result: 54 tumor samples harbor WES data in the clinical data table (exactly matching the paper's Figure 2b oncoprint caption of n=54). These are all samples with WES=='X' across all tumor types in MOESM4 (Atypical_NF=20, Neurofibroma=16, MPNST=15, Low-Grade MPNST=3). If restricted specifically to tumors labeled as MPNST (strict), n=15; if MPNST + Low-Grade MPNST are combined, n=18. The MAF file (MOESM5) contains mutation calls for 52 distinct Tumor_Sample_Barcodes (2 WES samples evidently had no reported mutations passing the reported filters and thus do not appear in the MAF).
denominator_used: N = 108 samples in the Clinical Data sheet (MOESM4) after filtering Tumor_ID non-null; WES=='X' yields 54 samples total (matching the paper's oncoprint n=54). MPNST-only: 16 total samples, 15 with WES. MPNST + Low-Grade MPNST: 19 total samples, 18 with WES. MAF (MOESM5) distinct Tumor_Sample_Barcode = 52.
assumptions_made: Figure 2b oncoprint 'n=54 whole exome sequencing' refers to all tumor types in the WES cohort, not only MPNSTs; the Clinical Data table column 'WES' with value 'X' denotes having WES data; samples listed in the MAF (52 distinct barcodes) may be a subset of the 54 WES samples (2 may have had no reportable mutations); the paper's 19 MPNSTs (16 MPNST + 3 Low-Grade MPNST) is the clinical MPNST group, while 'n=55 MPNSTs' referenced in the text may reflect a different grouping (e.g., including Atypical_NFs or an earlier cohort tally).
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- MOESM4 'Clinical Data' sheet: 108 rows x 15 cols (after Tumor_ID non-null filter); columns include Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, WES, WES label, Methylation, RNA-Seq.
- MOESM5 'Supplementary Table 2' (MAF): 7457 rows x multiple cols; 52 distinct Tumor_Sample_Barcode values.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- Filter 1: Tumor_ID non-null -> N=108 samples.
- Stratifications below use this full cohort.

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Tumor Type: {'Neurofibroma':35, 'Cutaneous_NF':33, 'Atypical_NF':21, 'MPNST':16, 'Low-Grade MPNST':3} = 108.
- WES indicator: 'X' (has WES) = 54; NaN = 54.
- Crosstab WES=='X' by Tumor Type: Atypical_NF=20, Neurofibroma=16, MPNST=15, Low-Grade MPNST=3, Cutaneous_NF=0; total=54.
- MAF distinct Tumor_Sample_Barcode = 52 (prefixed 'Tumor_').

D. COMPUTATION
- WES cohort total: (df['WES']=='X').sum() = 54.
- MPNST (strict) with WES: df[(df['Tumor Type']=='MPNST') & (df['WES']=='X')] -> 15 of 16.
- MPNST+LGM with WES: df[df['Tumor Type'].isin(['MPNST','Low-Grade MPNST']) & (df['WES']=='X')] -> 18 of 19.
- MAF distinct Tumor_Sample_Barcode: maf['Tumor_Sample_Barcode'].nunique() = 52.

E. RESULT
- 54 tumors harbor WES data (matches paper's Figure 2b oncoprint caption 'n = 54').
- Of those 54, only 15 are labeled MPNST (strict) and 18 are MPNST + Low-Grade MPNST.
- Mutation calls in the MAF cover 52 distinct samples.

F. CAVEATS
- The question text references two paper figures: the oncoprint (n=54) and Figure 2 legend/text (n=55 MPNSTs). The clinical data supports n=54 with WES as a total, but only 15-18 of those are labeled as MPNST/Low-Grade MPNST. The 55 MPNST count cited in the paper's text likely uses a broader histologic grouping (potentially including some Atypical_NF samples on the MPNST spectrum) not reflected in the discrete 'Tumor Type' column of MOESM4.
- The MAF has 52 distinct sample barcodes (2 fewer than 54); this is consistent with 2 samples having no reported mutations passing filtering thresholds and thus absent from the MAF. The count 54 is the definitive WES-data denominator per the supplied clinical table.
